title: Top K Frequent Elements
description:
Top K Frequent Elements

Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements. You may return the answer in any order.

Example 1

Input: nums = [1,1,1,2,2,3], k = 2
Output: [1,2]

Example 2

Input: nums = [1], k = 1
Output: [1]

Constraints

  • 1 <= nums.length <= 105
  • -104 <= nums[i] <= 104
  • k is in the range [1, number of unique elements in the array].
  • It is guaranteed that the answer is unique.

Follow-up: Your algorithm's time complexity must be better than O(n log n), where n is the array's size.
solution_text:
Approach: Bucket Sort

First build a frequency map counting how often each number appears. Then use bucket sort: create an array of buckets indexed by frequency (index i holds all numbers that appear exactly i times). The bucket array has at most n+1 slots.

Finally iterate the buckets from highest frequency down, collecting elements until we have k results. This runs in O(n) time — better than the O(n log n) heap approach.

Time: O(n)   Space: O(n)
